<<optimization of transportation network>>

the minimum spanning tree includes the edges a-b (5), b-e (3), d-e (1), c-d (2), and c-f (8). the total cost is 5 + 3 + 1 + 2 + 8 = 19. this tree connects all six distribution centers with the lowest possible transportation cost.

the shortest path from a to f is a-b-e-d-f, with a total cost of 5 + 3 + 1 + 6 = 15. this path ensures the lowest transportation cost while connecting the two distribution centers.
